matches.match_rationale: Salary grade and allowance logic is currently fragmented across campus-specific spreadsheets and payroll system configurations with no unified master schema. AI-assisted ETL can map these disparate structures into a canonical salary grade model, normalizing allowance types, pay band definitions, and role-to-grade mappings across all sites into a single authoritative dataset. This directly addresses the inconsistency root cause by creating a structured master from messy, semi-structured inputs.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A transformation pipeline that ingests salary spreadsheets and payroll exports from each campus, maps them to a unified grade/allowance schema, flags conflicts and unmapped entries for HR review, and produces a clean master configuration file that feeds back into the payroll system.

matches.match_rationale: When salary grades and allowances are inconsistently applied across hundreds of staff records, anomalous pay configurations — staff receiving wrong grade, duplicate allowances, or allowances not matching role — are hard to detect manually. An anomaly detection model trained on the expected pay structure can flag records that deviate from the norm for their role, campus, and tenure band, surfacing errors before payroll is processed. This directly targets the audit difficulty and inconsistency risk described in the pain point.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A monthly pre-payroll anomaly scan that compares every staff record's effective pay components against peer benchmarks by role and campus, generating a prioritized exception list for the payroll team to review and correct before disbursement.

matches.match_rationale: When a salary grade changes or a new allowance is introduced, the current pain is a cascade of manual recalculations across hundreds of records spanning multiple campuses — a multi-step, error-prone workflow. A task automation agent can orchestrate the end-to-end recalculation: reading the master grade table, identifying all affected staff records, computing new pay values, checking against policy rules, generating a change summary for HR approval, and writing approved changes back to the payroll system. This directly eliminates the manual re-work described in the pain point.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI agent triggered on grade/allowance change events that automatically propagates updates across all impacted staff records, produces a human-readable reconciliation report for sign-off, and submits approved changes to the payroll system — reducing a multi-day spreadsheet exercise to a supervised 30-minute workflow.

matches.match_rationale: Designing a unified salary grade structure that reconciles legacy campus-specific pay bands, contractual allowance entitlements, and budget caps is fundamentally a constraint satisfaction problem: the new master configuration must satisfy hard constraints (existing employment contracts, minimum wage floors, budget limits) and soft constraints (pay equity across campuses, internal grade ratios). AI constraint solvers can explore the configuration space and find valid unified structures that satisfy all constraints simultaneously — something spreadsheet-based trial and error cannot do efficiently.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A salary harmonization tool where HR inputs the constraints (contractual floors, budget envelope, equity targets) and the AI generates one or more valid unified grade/allowance configurations that satisfy all constraints, with a comparison view showing trade-offs between options.

matches.match_rationale: Salary grade and allowance logic currently hardcoded in spreadsheet formulas or ad-hoc payroll scripts is brittle, undocumented, and difficult to change consistently across sites. AI code generation can translate the business rules described in policy documents or HR briefs into clean, version-controlled payroll calculation functions — making the logic explicit, testable, and centrally maintainable rather than buried in spreadsheet cells across multiple campus files.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A code generation workflow where HR describes salary grade rules and allowance conditions in plain language, and the AI produces SQL or Python functions implementing those rules, with test cases covering edge cases — replacing fragile spreadsheet formulas with auditable, reusable code.
